evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Meta: Top Champions to Expect in Every Role
evidences.content: Corki:After several nerfs in recent patches, Corki's stock should have fallen. Enough to fall out of favor at Worlds 2025, though? We expect not, as the champion continues to be strong in lane and spikes hard with Trinity Force, providing the perfect window to fight for objectives. Kai'Sa:With several other ADCs on the roster taking a hit recently, Kai'Sa has risen as a prime pick just in time for Worlds. Not only can she poke while having great DPS, but her ultimate is also one of the best playmaking tools available to ADCs, and one the best players in the game are happy to exploit. [...] ### Top Lane: Ambessa:A staple in pro play ever since her release, Ambessa has pretty much everything a Top Laner wants: strong laning, backline access, mobility, self-reliance. At Worlds, Ambessa should remain a top priority pick early on in a series. Rumble:Riot's problem child in the Top Lane. Despite several nerfs throughout the year, Rumble continues to be a prime pick in pro play, all while his Solo Queue win rate remains in the gutter. Expect Rumble to see a high number of bans at Worlds as teams avoid having to deal with it. Jax:After receiving considerable buffs to his ultimate in Pat...

evidences.title: *NEW* CHANGES for PATCH 25.20! - WORLDS PATCH!
evidences.content: cap is being increased from 120 to 150. Veigar is going to be given some additional scaling power on W as the AP ratio is going up from 60 scaling to 100% to 70 scaling to 110%. Briar has been over-performing in recent patches, so she's going to be nerfed with her passive max stacks being lowered from seven to five. It really looks like Riot's trying to push Volibear away from his cosmic drive build because they're nerfing his AP ratios for a second time. Passive AP ratio is going down from 50 to 45% and E AP ratio is going to be lowered from 80 to 70%. Our challenger jungler Skillshot really does like his Black Cleaver rush on his Volibear, so that setup is going to become a little bit more enticing moving forward. Warwick is going to be slapped with two nerfs for this patch as his AD [...] There's a good chance Anivia becomes OP for solo queue due to these buffs, so definitely going to be a champ to watch out for in 25.20. Katarina is going to be receiving a base stat buff with her armor being increased from 28 to 32. So, those AD matchups do become a little better for her moving forward. Two buffs are penciled in for Malphite as his base armor is going up from 37 to 40 and th...

evidences.title: Patch 25.20 Notes
evidences.content: #### W - Seastone Trident Passive Damage over Time: 20 / 30 / 40 / 50 / 60 (+ 30% AP) ⇒ 30 / 45 / 60 / 75 / 90 (+ 25% AP) Tower Damage: Damage over time no longer affects structures We Did the Math: Damage over time now displays total damage to target Active Burst Damage: 50 / 70 / 90 / 110 / 130 ⇒ 50 / 75 / 100 / 125 / 150 On-Hit Damage: 10 / 15 / 20 / 25 / 30 (+ 35% AP) ⇒ 20 / 25 / 30 / 35 / 40 (+30% AP) #### R - Chum the Waters Guppy Damage: 150 / 250 / 350 (+ 80% AP) ⇒ 180 / 300 / 420 (+ 60% AP) Chomper Damage: 225 / 325 / 425 (+ 100% AP) ⇒ 225 / 375 / 525 (+ 75% AP) Gigalodon Damage: 300 / 400 / 500 (+ 120% AP) ⇒ 270 / 450 / 630 (+ 90% AP) [...] ## Banning Allied Hovers [NA & OCE ONLY] > In situations where your "teammate" bans your champion we see higher report rates, lower win rates, and a reported overall worse experience. In this patch we're experimenting with removing the ability to ban your ally's hovered champion to help alleviate these negative interactions. We'll be watching how this change goes and listening to your feedback before making a call on whether we revert this change or roll it out worldwide, so please let us know what you think! evidences.title: Undefeated KT vs Unshakeable GEN | Worlds 2025
evidences.content: # Undefeated KT vs Unshakeable GEN | Worlds 2025 ## LoL Esports 3920000 subscribers 6830 likes ### Description 241335 views Posted: 1 Nov 2025 The last time KT Rolster and GEN.G met, the world expected dominance, but reality shattered prediction. What was supposed to be a routine win for GEN turned into a 3–2 banger from BDD and KT, proving that even giants bleed. Now, on the Worlds 2025 stage, the stakes are higher and the spotlight is blinding. KT arrive undefeated, not a single game dropped. BDD looks like the best mid laner in the tournament, and the dreaded “KT quarterfinal curse” is finally dead. [...] But standing across from them is a roster built to win everything. MSI champions. Back-to-back LCK champions. Five of the most dominant players in the world, Chovy, Ruler, Canyon, Duro, and Kiin, all locked on one mission: bring the Summoner’s Cup back to Korea, this time, with them. KT fight to prove this is not a miracle run, it’s their moment. GEN fight to claim what many would consider to be rightfully theirs. This is where legends break… or become eternal. This is Worlds 2025. Earn your legacy. #lolesports #leagueoflegends #Worlds2025 Play-Ins: Oct 14 Swiss Stage: Oct 1...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Primer - LoL Esports
evidences.content: Tier 1 vs Tier 3 Tier 2 vs Tier 2 And of course: no same-region showdowns in Round 1 Swiss Rounds 2–5 (October 15, 16, 19, 24) From here on out, it’s simple: Teams face others with the same record (1-0 vs 1-0, 0-1 vs 0-1, etc.). No repeat opponents. If a clash would repeat, or if a draw would force a different matchup to repeat, the team is redrawn into the next open slot. The Knockout Draw (October 25) Eight teams will rise from the Swiss Stage: 2 teams at 3-0 3 teams at 3-1 3 teams at 3-2 Quarterfinals are decided live: Each 3-0 team draws a 3-2 opponent and is placed on opposite sides of the bracket. The rest are drawn in sequence—no restrictions, no protections, just pure bracket fate. Image 40 DROPS [...] Play-In Stage (Bo5) - Beijing(All matches begin at 4 p.m. CST / 10 a.m. CET / 1 a.m. PT unless otherwise noted) Tuesday, October 14 Swiss Stage (Bo1, Bo3)- Beijing (All matches begin at 4 p.m. CST / 10 a.m. CET / 1 a.m. PT unless otherwise noted) Wednesday, October 15 - 1 p.m. CST / 7 a.m. CET / 10 p.m. PT Oct. 14 Thursday, October 16 - 1 p.m. CST / 7 a.m. CET / 10 p.m. PT Oct. 15 Friday, October 17 Saturday, October 18 Sunday, October 19 Wednesday, October 22 Thursday, Oc...

evidences.title: *NEW* TIER LIST for PATCH 25.20 - THE WORLDS PATCH! - League of Legends
evidences.content: for this patch as we've got Morgana, Diana, and Leblanc in the OP tier. Since 25.20 is the patch that worlds will be played on, Riot will most likely be nerfing Leblanc and Morgana for 25.21. So, definitely take advantage of them while you can. Both champs were overbuffed for solo Q because Riot wants them to be higher priority in proplay at this year's world, so they shouldn't be in the OP tier for too much longer. If you don't want to ban out Leblanc right now, then pulling out Malahar or Vex are two really good answers to her. Morgana is pretty difficult to counter because she can just brainlessly spam W on the wave and not have to interact at all in lane. But Zed and Auction are two champs who can play a bit better than most into the pick due to being AD and also having longrange [...] W AP ratio being increased by 10% at all ranks. As we can see in the comparison clips, this will equate to around a 23 damage increase per W when you're on your two item spike. Likely not enough to propel Vaggar up into an S tier spot, but he will be placed in A as a good mid-lane pickup. Oriana is on the nerf list for this patch as her W base damage is going down from 70 scaling to 270 to 70...

evidences.content: Retrouvez le règlement officiel des Worlds 2025 ici. Voici tout ce qu'il faut savoir ! Les Worlds se déroulent en trois étapes : les play-ins (14 octobre), la phase à système suisse (15 - 25 octobre) et la phase finale (28 octobre - 9 novembre). Play-ins (BO5) - Pékin (Tous les matchs démarrent à 10h CEST, sauf mention contraire) Mardi 14 octobre Phase à système suisse (BO1, BO3) - Pékin (Tous les matchs démarrent à 10h CEST, sauf mention contraire) Mercredi 15 octobre - 7h CET Jeudi 16 octobre - 7h CEST Vendredi 17 octobre Samedi 18 octobre Dimanche 19 octobre Mercredi 22 octobre Jeudi 23 octobre Vendredi 24 octobre Samedi 25 octobre - 7h CEST Phase finale (BO5) - Shanghai et Chengdu (Tous les matchs commencent à 8h CET sauf indication contraire) [...] PSG Talon (PSG) FORMAT DES WORLDS TIRAGE DE LA PHASE À SYSTÈME SUISSE Le tirage pour la phase à système suisse aura lieu tout de suite après les play-ins. Les duels seront annoncés en direct, pour lancer les premières rivalités aux Worlds. Manche 1 (14 octobre) La première manche repose entièrement sur les placements. Cette année, nous avons modifié les paliers du tirage et rajouté une nouvelle règle : le vainqueur des play-ins,...

evidences.title: LoL Esports |
evidences.content: We want to reward consistency and good performance throughout the season while building up towards the climax in Split 3 as teams get to rack up increasingly more points each Split. Moving on to format, we will be retaining our structure from 2025 for Splits 1 and 2, and introduce Swiss Stage in Split 3. Here’s what each Split’s format looks like. Split 1 Teams compete in a Single Round-Robin Best-of-3 (BO3) format during the Regular Season. The top 6 teams will then advance to the Knockout Stage and battle through a series of hybrid elimination Best-of-5 (BO5) matches to become LCP’s representative at First Stand 2026. Split 2 In the Regular Season of Split 2, teams face off in a Single Round-Robin BO3 to determine the top 6 teams moving on to the Knockout Stage. [...] All final seeding matches will be BO5s Used to determine the 1st placed team + This team will be allowed to choose who they face in the Playoffs between the 3rd and 4th placed teams Tie-breaker between any teams tied after 3 losses e.g. 1-3, 2-3 + Winner of this tie-breaker will get 5 additional Championship Points (on top of what they would get for their Swiss Stage results) Next, in the Playoffs, the 4 teams th...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Heads to China! - LoL Esports
evidences.content: Worlds 2025 begins with 17 teams vying for the trophy. The Play-In Stage will kick off October 14 in Beijing at the state-of-the-art Beijing Smart Esports Center, a venue built specifically for premier esports events. This will be the first time the Beijing Smart Esports Center hosts a World Championship, and it promises to deliver an electric atmosphere. New this year, the Play-In Stage will host a one-day best of five between the fourth seeded team of the two best performing regions from MSI to punch their ticket to the Swiss Stage. From October 15-25, the Swiss Stage will bring together 16 teams from across the globe in a dynamic format, where every win moves you closer to knockout contention—and every loss brings you closer to elimination. [...] Riot Games League of Legends © 2026 Riot Games. All Rights Reserved. Privacy NoticeTerms of ServiceAccessibility The 2025 League of Legends World Championship is heading back to China and will take place from October 14 to November 9. Fans around the world can look forward to world-class competition, unforgettable moments, and the crowning of the next World Champion. ### Play-In and Swiss Stages – Beijing Smart Esports Center, Beijin...

evidences.title: LoL Esports |
evidences.content: Movistar R7 (R7) ## What’s the format? This year, Worlds will feature 20 teams in three stages of competition: the Play-In Stage, the Swiss Stage, and the Knockout Stage (Quarterfinals, Semifinals, and Finals). A detailed breakdown of each stage is below. Play-In Stage: Sept. 25-29 [...] Swiss Stage: Oct. 3-7; Oct. 10-13 The four qualified Play-In Stage teams will join the top 2 seeded teams from LEC and LCS as well as the top 4 seeded teams from the LCK and LPL. These 16 teams will be split into four draw pools and will be drawn into an initial set of 8 best-of-one matches based on their seeding: Pool 1 teams will play Pool 4 teams, and Pool 2 teams will play Pool 3 teams. Additionally, no team will play a team from their own region in the first round. Pools consist of: Pool 1: LCK1, LCS1, LEC1, LPL1 Pool 2: LCK2, LPL2, LCS2, LEC2 Pool 3: LCK3, LCK4, LPL3, LPL4 Pool 4: Play-In Stage Teams [...] Pick’Em, Powered by AWS, opens a week before Worlds begins with both Crystal Ball and Play-In Stage predictions. Crystal Ball challenges players to dive deep into the meta, making tournament-long predictions that are scored separately from traditional Pick’Em picks. Swiss Picks: 1 p.m. P...

evidences.title: MSI and Worlds Updates - LoL Esports
evidences.content: Format Update: The format for Worlds 2026 will largely mirror Worlds 2025, with updates limited to the Play-Ins format and regional seeding. As part of these updates, we’ve reviewed the regional allocations for Worlds 2026 and will grant CBLOL an additional slot at the event. With this addition, Worlds will feature 19 teams from around the world competing in the United States. A total of 19 teams will qualify: 3 teams each from LCK, LCS, LCP, LEC, and LPL (15 total) 2 teams from CBLOL 1 team: MSI Champion\ 1 additional team from the second-place region at MSI [...] Format Update: The format for MSI 2026 will look very similar to last year, with the only changes occurring in the Play-In Stage. The Play-In Stage will feature a 4-team Double-Elimination Bracket that will qualify a single team into the Bracket Stage. The 7 teams that will skip the Play-Ins and start in the Bracket Stage are the 1 Seed from each region, as well as the second seed of the highest ranking two seed region from First Stand. Eleven teams will qualify: 2 Teams each from LCK, LCS, LCP, LEC, and LPL (10 total) 1 team from CBLOL Ticket Sales Information Tickets for MSI 2026 will be released in waves through NO...

evidences.title: LoL Esports in 2025
evidences.content: Campeonato Mundial de 2025 O Mundial acontecerá em algumas das cidades mais icônicas da China. A Fase de Entrada e a Fase Suíça darão início ao campeonato em Pequim, enquanto as Quartas de Final e as Semifinais acontecerão em Xangai, culminando com a Grande Final em Chengdu. Vamos compartilhar mais detalhes sobre locais do evento em breve, mas uma novidade desse ano que já podemos anunciar é a eliminação da semana de intervalo entre as Quartas de Final e as Semifinais. Muitos fãs compartilharam que sentem que o Mundial perde o embalo durante esse período, então fizemos mudanças e estamos empolgados para apresentar uma fase atrás da outra, sem deixar que a ação pare. [...] O Campeonato Mundial contará com 17 equipes: 3 vagas por região, 1 vaga para a região com o segundo melhor desempenho no MSI 2025 e 1 vaga para a região da Campeã do MSI 2025. Voltaremos ainda este ano para compartilhar mais informações sobre o formato completo do Mundial. Drops em 2025 Os drops voltarão para o LoL Esports em 2025, durante a temporada regular e grandes eventos como First Stand, MSI e o Campeonato Mundial. Embora os drops sejam aleatórios e possam surgir a qualquer momento em uma partida, alguns...

evidences.content: CHINA — LPL Bilibili Gaming (BLG) Top Esports (TES) COREIA — LCK Hanwha Life Esports (HLE) T1 (T1) EMEA — LEC G2 Esports (G2) Karmine Corp (KC) AMÉRICA DO NORTE — LCS LYON (LYON) Team Liquid Alienware (TLAW) ÁSIA-PACÍFICO — LCP Team Secret Whales (TSW) Revolve Deep Cross Gaming (DCG) BRASIL — CBLOL Furia (FUR) ### FORMATO O formato do MSI 2026 será muito parecido com o do MSI 2025, com as únicas mudanças ocorrendo na Fase de Entrada. Este ano, a Fase de Entrada contará com uma Chave de Eliminação Dupla de 4 equipes, classificando uma única equipe na Fase Eliminatória. A Fase Eliminatória continuará sendo uma Chave de Eliminação Dupla de 8 equipes. ### DROPS
